title: salix × aschersoniana
intro: Salix × aschersoniana, also known as Weeping Willow or Ascherson's Willow, is a hybrid plant species in the willow family (Salicaceae) that is a cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its salicin content. The health benefits of Salix × aschersoniana include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, and its use in treating conditions such as fever, arthritis, and digestive issues. Salix × aschersoniana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
